mechanism_narrative: A stable or rising FEDFUNDS rate above the current sub-1% level keeps expected short rates anchored upward, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days as markets reprice the policy path. The signal fails if inflation and activity weaken enough to pull rate-cut expectations forward despite unchanged FEDFUNDS.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d Treasury-yield changes are negative in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS is flat or rising, or if the effect disappears once CPIAUCSL and UNRATE are controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates by at least 0.3% over three months, breakeven inflation and term premium expectations rise, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails when the CPI move is driven by temporary base effects that reverse within one month.
failure_condition: Reject if yield rises are not more frequent after CPIAUCSL acceleration events than after non-events, or if the sign flips negative when CPIAUCSL is above trend but decelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration, increasing recession odds and future policy easing expectations, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days. The signal is not active when payroll growth remains strong despite a higher unemployment rate.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor-weakness episodes do not produce negative 20d yield changes, or if the relationship vanishes once PAYEMS growth is not deteriorating.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in RSAFS above its recent pace signals stronger consumption demand, lifting growth expectations and nominal yield pressure so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days. The effect fails if sales gains are offset by falling prices or if consumer demand is weakening elsewhere.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS growth does not precede positive 20d yield moves more often than chance, or if the sign is neutral during periods of weak labor and production data.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST remains weak, real activity is improving without enough housing-supply relief, which supports stronger nominal growth and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if industrial gains are isolated and do not broaden into demand conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition is followed by negative 20d yield changes in a majority of cases, or if INDPRO alone explains the effect without HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly upswing in GDPC1 signals stronger aggregate demand and better growth expectations, which raises the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days as investors price less policy easing. The signal fails if GDP growth is already expected and fully priced before the release.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 surprises are not followed by higher yields on average, or if the effect is confined to one quarter and disappears in subsequent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, the labor market is cooling and rate-cut expectations increase, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if payroll weakness occurs alongside accelerating inflation that offsets the growth scare.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth turns negative and UNRATE rises >= 0.1pp
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-slowdown condition does not predict lower 20d yields, or if high CPIAUCSL inflation cancels the effect in most triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a higher FEDFUNDS rate combined with firm CPIAUCSL keeps real policy expectations restrictive, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 5 trading days as the curve reprices faster hikes. The signal fails outside tightening regimes or when inflation is falling rapidly.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes do not produce positive 5d yield changes, or if the relationship reverses when CPIAUCSL is decelerating.
